title: Image works in docker but not in podman
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
There seems to be some critical difference in podman and docker defaults, that makes an image work in docker but not podman. Doing ```podman run -ti docker.io/wickr/bot-cloud:5.92.06.01``` has the container output nodejs version strings and then exits/dies. Doing ```docker run -ti docker.io/wickr/bot-cloud:5.92.06.01``` successfully launches the container to a welcome screen.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run ```podman run -ti docker.io/wickr/bot-cloud:5.92.06.01```

2. Watch it fail.

**Describe the results you received:**
Container prints which nodejs version it is running and then dies/exits.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container runs and shows a welcome screen after the initial node version and other setup prints.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I have tried using privileged=true, running podman as sudo, forcing drivers to match what I observe as docker defaults (json-file for log driver, overlay2 for storage driver), and whatever other run options I thought that could be the culprit. So far, to no avail. Both podman logs and journalctl showed nothing helpful, though not a master of either.

I poked around the run script, and the step following the nodejs version is related to shuffling some sql files around. As my expectation was for podman to work, I did my first tries as more proper, doing a mount with ```-v mydir:/opt/WickrIO```. Checking ```mydir```, I can see some database files there. The run script has some echos after the file shuffle, but those never show up. Makes me think this could be filesystem related. Or maybe there is some stop signal problem and the script triggers what podman thinks is an exit.

Note: I do not work for Wickr, just poked around the run script.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.17.5
Built:        Wed May 25 07:40:16 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.1.0-4.el9.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes to troubleshooting, no to latest as the latest is not available on CentOS 9 Stream. Checked the release notes for was it 4.2 that is latest, and didn't see changes that would fix this.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Virtual server.

comments.body:
This is not what I am seeing. In rootless mode I see:
```
$ podman run -ti docker.io/wickr/bot-cloud:5.92.06.01
Trying to pull docker.io/wickr/bot-cloud:5.92.06.01...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d86264fcda4d done  
Copying blob ac932d510bbd done  
Copying blob a309513fcc1e done  
Copying blob bd14aaf84cbd done  
Copying blob 3d4af735f98f done  
Copying blob cf06a7c31611 done  
Copying blob 2a0cb699a3d6 done  
Copying blob 948054364aa6 done  
Copying blob 857699735d37 done  
Copying blob f5763975ea4f done  
Copying blob 4726d79f909a done  
Copying blob 7140cd2a695f done  
Copying config 5d955b8791 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Now using node v12.20.2 (npm v6.14.11)
Updating integration software version numbers
Updating integration software version numbers
Searching NPM registry

Welcome to the WickrIO Console program (v5.92.6.1)

This console is used to maintain the WickrIO bot clients. The commands of this
program support adding, modifying, deleting, starting and stopping the WickrIO
bot clients. Before you add a WickrIO bot client to this system you will have
to use the Wickr Admin Console to create the client. Once you have done that
you can use the 'add' command to add it to this system. You will need to select
one of the integrations.
You can modify the integration software after installation. Details on how to
create a bot integration are documented in the WickrIO Bot Integration guide.
...
Continue to see welcome message on startup? (default: yes):
```
I am on Fedora 36 with podman 4.1.

Interesting. I wonder if the problem could be that as it was a fresh server for testing the account logged in was the root and not a "regular" admin user account? I can try with an admin user later today if you think that could be it.
Can you try it again or in rootful mode?
What is the proper way for rootful mode: sudo podman, passing -root, doing -priviliged=true, or a combination of these? Want to make sure I test the right way.
fails in rootful for me too ... rootless works. looking for a reason

I managed to get rootless to fail too. If I mount a directory not owned by the current user (e.g. owned by root), the process exits with code 1 after printing the nodejs stuff. If I mount a directory owned by the current user/group (creating new dir in user home), the container works.

Example: ```podman run --name wickr -v /mnt/apps/opt/wickr-io:/opt/WickrIO -ti wickr/bot-cloud:5.92.06.01``` fails (/mnt/apps/opt/wickr-io owned by root, but drwxrwxrwx permissions), but ```podman run --name wickr -v ~/wickr:/opt/WickrIO -ti wickr/bot-cloud:5.92.06.01``` works (just mkdir in home folder as current user).

I can probably work around this issue, but would be nice to kind of know why and where it fails, or get like a helpful error message somewhere.
If this is an SELinux system, then SELinux would block the access. Try with a :Z.

No need, sorted the issue out. I had forgotten to nuke the files created by the initial attempt, and those were owned and only modifiable by root. The rootless now works without a hitch.

As the proper use will be running on a dedicated user and group, the rootful not working doesn't really matter to me. Feel free to keep the issue open if you think there's something to it though (maybe some default setting in rootful is wrong).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
